private.name: Tenna Systems

data.narrative:
Distributed Spectrum is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public companies operating in the same sector and stage, including peers such as Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $62M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Tenna Systems at $60M (Seed), Ares Industries at $150M (Series A), Vatn Systems at $300M (Series A), and Stark Defence at $500M (Series A). The median across this set is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $53M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $144M. The wide spread reflects meaningful stage dispersion within the peer set—the inclusion of Series A companies materially elevates the median relative to stage-progression alone. Key uncertainties center on Distributed Spectrum's specific market position relative to Series A-stage comparables and whether current traction supports a valuation closer to the Seed baseline ($53M–$62M) or the broader peer median ($144M).
